text: I sense a calm contrast between the clinical sterility of the empty meeting room and the vibrant stimulation of the R+D display. In the first space, I feel slightly uneasy—its formality and emptiness invite introspection, as if I’m lingering at the threshold of purpose and anticipation. In the second, surrounded by color, light, and ideas, curiosity takes hold. Questions about innovation, connection, and the future of human interaction swirl in my mind. I feel both observed and an observer, part of the ongoing experiment of our relation to technology and meaning.
